# Python LLM Voice Assistant 🎙️🤖

A hands-free, voice-activated AI assistant built with Python. By integrating Google's Gemini Flash model with speech recognition and text-to-speech libraries, this script allows you to have real-time, spoken conversations with an AI named "Atlas" directly from your terminal.

## Features
* **Speech-to-Text (STT):** Uses the `SpeechRecognition` library to capture audio from your microphone and convert it to text in real-time.
* **Text-to-Speech (TTS):** Employs `pyttsx3` to give the AI a voice, reading out the responses generated by the LLM.
* **Ambient Noise Handling:** Automatically adjusts the microphone sensitivity to filter out background noise (like fans or AC) before listening.
* **Gemini LLM Integration:** Powered by the `google-genai` SDK, offering fast and intelligent responses using the `gemini-flash-latest` model.
* **Continuous Chat:** Maintains a continuous conversation loop until you say "quit" or "exit."

## Tech Stack
* **Language:** [Python 3](https://www.python.org/)
* **AI Model:** Google Gemini (`google-genai`)
* **Audio Processing:** `SpeechRecognition`, `pyttsx3`, `PyAudio`

## Prerequisites
Before you begin, ensure you have the following installed on your machine:
* **Python 3.x:** Download and install from [python.org](https://www.python.org/downloads/). *(Note: If using Python 3.13+, you may need to uncomment the `audioop_lts` patch at the top of the script).*
* **Microphone:** A working microphone connected to your system.
* **API Key:** A valid Google Gemini API key. You can get one from Google AI Studio.

## Requirements
To run this project, you will need the following Python packages installed.
<pre>
SpeechRecognition
pyttsx3
PyAudio
python-dotenv
google-genai
</pre>

## Installation & Setup
Follow these steps to get the project running on your local machine:

1. **Clone the repository:** Download the project files to your computer using Git.
2. **Navigate to the directory:** Change your terminal's current directory to the newly cloned project folder.
3. **Create a virtual environment (Recommended):** This keeps the project's dependencies isolated from your system's global Python packages.
4. **Activate the environment:** Turn on the virtual environment so that packages are installed locally. *(Note: The command differs slightly for Windows).*
5. **Install dependencies:** Use `pip` to install all necessary packages listed in the requirements section.

## Directory structure:
<pre>
py-llm-assistant/
  └── main.py
</pre>
